ogTitle: Swans Crossing vs Swansea: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Swans Crossing and Swansea.
heading: Swans Crossing vs Swansea — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Swans Crossing, New South Wales and Swansea, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Swans Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Swansea (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Swans Crossing or Swansea safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Swans Crossing has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Swansea scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Swans Crossing scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Swansea scores 17/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Swans Crossing or Swansea?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Swans Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
